statement: The Convex provider execution layer can be deleted from the live engine path, leaving only shared provider/model registry definitions that are still used by schemas and worker APIs.
rationale: Temporal now owns provider side effects, and current repo evidence shows the Convex execution actions are not referenced outside tests.
keyAssumptions: No hidden operator or deployment path still uses provider_services actions.
Provider/model registries remain needed for shared data validation.
validationCriteria: Ripgrep confirms no non-test runtime call sites for provider execution actions.
Worker/runtime code continues to compile after separating the registry from execution code.
critiquePoints: Deleting too aggressively could break undocumented maintenance flows.
questions: Should the remaining registry move to engine-settings or stay in engine-convex as the data-plane source of truth?
